Name: latency_optimized_inference
Title: Latency-Optimized Inference on Commodity GPUs via Profile-Guided Kernel Engineering
Short Hypothesis: Carefully optimized kernel-level throughput engineering can lead to significant latency reductions and improved tokens-per-second on commodity GPUs for deep learning inference, without relying on theoretical bounds or complex mathematical guarantees.
Related Work: Recent works such as Spectral Latent Steering, Spectral Quantization Routing, and Geometric Signal Separation focus on test-time optimization, mixed-precision inference, and geometric signal separation, respectively. However, these approaches often rely on theoretical guarantees, heuristic control mechanisms, or complex mathematical formulations. In contrast, this proposal focuses on empirical, profile-guided kernel engineering to optimize latency and throughput on commodity GPUs.
Abstract: This proposal aims to investigate the potential of profile-guided kernel engineering for optimizing deep learning inference on commodity GPUs. By leveraging kernel-level throughput engineering, memory-bandwidth utilization, and tensor-core occupancy, we seek to reduce latency and improve tokens-per-second without relying on complex theoretical guarantees. Our approach will involve empirically driven experiments, nvprof/Nsight traces, and a focus on measured wall-clock latency and tokens-per-second on real hardware. We will evaluate our approach on a range of deep learning models and tasks, reporting p50/p99 latency, throughput under batching, and energy per token.
Experiments: Optimize kernel launch configurations for maximum throughput and minimum latency
Investigate the impact of memory-bandwidth utilization on inference latency
Evaluate the effects of tensor-core occupancy on tokens-per-second
Compare the performance of different deep learning models and tasks under optimized kernel configurations
Measure p50/p99 latency, throughput under batching, and energy per token for each experiment
Risk Factors and Limitations: The proposal relies on empirical experiments and may not generalize to all deep learning models or tasks
The optimized kernel configurations may not be portable across different GPU architectures or driver versions
The focus on latency and tokens-per-second may compromise model accuracy or other performance metrics
